message:
fix(mcp-client): replace sentinel file hack with proper empty-path handling in discovery

The previous implementation used a non-existent sentinel file path
(NO_DEFAULT_DISCOVERY_SENTINEL) passed to ConfigLoader::from_path when
include_default_paths=false, relying on it silently failing. This was
fragile and unclear.

Now:
- Early-return with empty Vec when both include_default_paths=false AND
  discovery_paths is empty (no servers to discover)
- When include_default_paths=false but discovery_paths is non-empty,
  seed ConfigLoader::from_path with the first explicit path, then
  add remaining paths — no sentinel file needed
- Added structured tracing for the empty-discovery case

Also syncs beads issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-1u90p.8.6","title":"Execute final go/no-go review and archive evidence bundle for sign-off","description":"## Context
Final release promotion requires a single authoritative evidence package and decision record.

## Scope
Execute formal go/no-go review using objective evidence from correctness, performance, reliability, and user-perception tracks.

## Deliverables
- Consolidated evidence bundle (unit/integration/e2e/perf/soak outputs)
- Risk register with residual risks and mitigation ownership
- Signed decision record with explicit release rationale and rollback readiness

## Why this matters
Ensures release quality and accountability are evidence-driven, not impression-driven.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Evidence bundle includes pass/fail summaries and links to detailed artifacts for all required tracks.
- No unresolved critical blocker remains; exceptions are explicitly approved and documented.
- Rollback plan, kill-switch verification, and on-call readiness are confirmed.
- Decision record references beta feedback and SLO compliance data.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Complete go/no-go review with an authoritative evidence bundle spanning correctness, performance, reliability, and user-perceived smoothness gates.
2. Require explicit sign-off criteria: no unresolved critical blockers, documented exceptions with approvers, and validated rollback/kill-switch readiness.
3. Require comprehensive validation references: unit tests, integration tests, end-to-end scripts, benchmarks, and soak outcomes tied to release decision thresholds.
4. Require structured diagnostics/logging and artifact index for every evidence source so the decision is fully auditable and reproducible.
5. Closure requires signed decision record, archived artifacts, on-call readiness confirmation, and residual-risk ownership map.","notes":"Release-governance control point: this bead is a hard gate, not a summary task. Keep evidence traceability and rollback readiness explicit so operator confidence is data-backed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:48.654838Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T20:17:51.260305Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","governance","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","reflow","release","resize","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.7.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.7.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","type":"blocks","create  {"id":"ft-1u90p.8.7","title":"Run controlled beta feedback loop with user-perceived smoothness telemetry","description":"## Context
Lab metrics alone can miss user-perception failures.

## Scope
Add a structured beta loop combining telemetry and qualitative feedback to validate perceived smoothness and stability before broad rollout.

## Deliverables
- Beta cohort plan and feedback taxonomy
- Telemetry-to-feedback correlation dashboard
- Decision rubric for promotion/rollback between rollout stages

## Why this matters
Ensures release decisions reflect real user outcomes, not just internal benchmark wins.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Beta runs collect statistically useful telemetry and categorized feedback.
- Promotion decisions cite both objective SLOs and perception signals.
- Findings are documented and reflected in final release guidance.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Execute controlled beta loop with explicit unit/integration/e2e evidence references tied to user-perceived smoothness and reliability outcomes.
2. Collect structured telemetry and user feedback cohorts with deterministic sampling and reproducible analysis artifacts.
3. Validate rollback/mitigation playbooks via end-to-end test scripts before broad rollout actions are approved.
4. Document anomaly/failure taxonomy with triage owners and close-loop remediation tracking.
5. Closure requires auditable evidence package demonstrating readiness for final go/no-go decisions.","notes":"Dependency rationale: controlled beta is gated by compatibility contract, alt-screen conformance, soak stability, telemetry dashboards, and canary plan so user-facing exposure starts only after high-risk regressions are screened  {"id":"ft-1u90p.9","title":"Latency-immunity architecture contract for local-first input/reflow under remote mux pressure","description":"## Goal
Design and codify a latency-immunity architecture so FrankenTerm remains locally responsive for text entry and resize/font-change reflow even when remote mux servers are CPU-saturated, swapping, or IO-stalled.

## Problem Framing
Observed pathological mode in legacy mux environments:
- remote mux CPU saturation + swap pressure + storage chunk exhaustion
- feedback loop: output flood -> mux lag -> backlog growth -> more stalls
- user-visible result: typing lag, resize hitching, reflow jank

## Scope
1. Define local-first control plane boundaries:
   - keep input/resize admission and scheduling decisions local
   - decouple remote ingest lag from local UI interaction budgets
2. Define bounded backpressure and degradation ladder:
   - always preserve input and viewport reflow budgets
   - demote/drop non-critical background work first
3. Define formal invariants and SLO contracts:
   - p95/p99 typing latency, resize stage budgets, stall age caps
4. Define implementation slices aligned to existing modules:
   - resize_scheduler / viewport_reflow_planner / backpressure_severity / wezterm adapter paths
5. Define evidence and rollout gates:
   - benchmark/proptest/e2e/soak/fault-injection artifact requirements

## Mandatory Constraints
- No regressions in terminal semantics or safety policy behavior.
- All heavy compile/test/benchmark operations must run via `rch exec -- ...`.
- Favor reversible feature flags and explicit rollback criteria.

## Initial Deliverable in This Bead
A concrete architecture contract document with:
- threat model and failure taxonomy
- recommendation contracts (EV-scored) using alien-graveyard primitives
- first implementation sequence with measurable exit criteria

## Acceptance Criteria
- Architecture contract committed under docs with explicit invariants and rollout gates.
- At least 3 EV-scored recommendation cards (>=2.0) with fallback triggers.
- Bead graph updated with downstream implementation tasks/dependencies.
- Coordination updates attempted via agent mail; if transport fails, blocker is documented.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:02:10.694933Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T22:02:10.694933Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","performance","quality-gate","reflow","resize","test-coverage","test-logging","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:02:10.694933Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-1u90p.9.1","title":"Input-preserving backpressure hardening (reserve floors + S3-FIFO admission)","description":"Implement Card C from `docs/latency-immunity-architecture-ft-1u90p.9.md`.

Scope:
- enforce hard local interaction reserve floors in frame budget accounting
- ensure cold scrollback/background work is first to throttle/drop
- integrate S3-FIFO-style admission/eviction policy where queue/cache pressure is currently FIFO/LRU-like
- emit explicit stale/drop markers and structured diagnostics

Validation:
- unit tests for reserve-floor invariants
- integration tests under synthetic remote-domain pressure
- e2e scenario proving no local input starvation during remote stall windows
- run heavy checks via `rch exec -- ...`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:44.612051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:44.612051Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backpressure","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","performance","reflow","resize","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.1","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:44.612051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-1u90p.9.2","title":"Local snapshot fast path (seqlock-style reads + epoch reclamation)","description":"Implement Card A from `docs/latency-immunity-architecture-ft-1u90p.9.md`.

Scope:
- add local snapshot fast path for interaction-critical scheduler/read surfaces
- use versioned optimistic reads (seqlock-style) for low-latency reads
- use epoch-based reclamation/deferred cleanup for old snapshots
- preserve terminal semantic correctness and safe fallback behavior

Validation:
- concurrency/property tests for no torn-visible reads and no stale UAF access
- deterministic replay proving behavior equivalence on visible outputs
- structured telemetry for snapshot retry/fallback rates
- run heavy checks via `rch exec -- ...`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:45.507516Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:57.559561Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","performance","reflow","resize","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:45.507516Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:57.559516Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-1u90p.9.3","title":"Remote health isolation for latency immunity (SWIM suspicion + sequential declaration)","description":"Implement Card B from `docs/latency-immunity-architecture-ft-1u90p.9.md`.

Scope:
- introduce remote health classifier (`healthy/flaky/failed`) and isolate remote domains from local interaction budgets
- integrate SWIM-style suspicion signals into domain budgeting path
- add sequential declaration guardrails to reduce false positives
- maintain deterministic conservative fallback under uncertainty

Validation:
- fault-injection scenarios for partitions, delayed acks, and remote overload
- calibration report for false-positive/false-negative behavior
- end-to-end test proving local input/reflow SLOs remain within budget while remote domain degrades
- run heavy checks via `rch exec -- ...`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:46.351520Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:58.451958Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","performance","reflow","resize","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:46.351520Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:58.451899Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-1u90p.9.4","title":"Latency-immunity fault-injection + go/no-go evidence pack","description":"Operational proof and release gating for `ft-1u90p.9`.

Scope:
- implement lineage-driven fault injection scenarios targeting latency-immunity invariants
- build evidence bundle format for GO/HOLD/ROLLBACK on this track
- wire telemetry-to-decision checkpoints into rollout review docs

Validation:
- reproducible fault campaign artifacts
- deterministic replay for every discovered violation
- no unresolved critical latency-immunity blockers before promotion","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:47.154728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T22:05:00.674353Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","performance","quality-gate","reflow","resize","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.4","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:47.154728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.4","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:04:59.332072Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.4","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:05:00.070859Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.9.4","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:05:00.674301Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-1u9qw","title":"Protocol-versioned local IPC namespace and compatibility handshake","description":"Goal: adopt contract-versioned local socket namespace and explicit compatibility handshake for FrankenTerm local IPC (robot and native). Why: prevent client/server skew from attaching to incompatible sessions. Scope: version local IPC endpoints by protocol or schema version, add handshake with explicit mismatch error payload (expected and actual version), define compatibility policy, and add integration tests for mismatch and upgrade. Deliverables: IPC versioning spec, endpoint discovery implementation changes, and compatibility tests with operator-visible diagnostics. Acceptance criteria: incompatible clients rejected deterministically with stable error code; compatible clients connect without behavior regressions; robot and native workflows keep stable semantics across patch upgrades. Cross-references: wa-vcjbi, wa-3kxe, wa-dr6zv, wa-3dfxb.13.","notes":"wa-1u9qw implementation now fully re-applied and validated in workspace.
Changes completed:
1) crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs
- Added protocol constants: IPC_CONTRACT_DIR_NAME=contract_version_1, IPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION=1, IPC_PROTOCOL_MISMATCH_CODE=ipc.protocol_mismatch.
- Added IpcEnvelope.protocol_version with serde default for legacy requests.
- Server rejects protocol mismatch or missing protocol version with stable code ipc.protocol_mismatch and expected vs actual message.
- Auth failures now return stable code ipc.auth_denied.
- Client includes protocol_version in every IPC envelope.
- Added tests: ipc_envelope_includes_protocol_version and ipc_rejects_protocol_mismatch.
2) crates/frankenterm-core/src/config.rs
- Default ipc.socket_path now uses IPC_SOCKET_NAME constant.
- Relative IPC socket paths now resolve under .ft/contract_version_1.
- Added tests for namespaced relative path and absolute-path passthrough.
3) crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs
- IPC pane-state helper now surfaces error_code with detail in returned error string.
Validation completed:
- rustfmt --edition 2024 on changed files: pass.
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --target-dir target_emerald: pass.
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib ipc::tests::ipc_envelope_includes_protocol_version --target-dir target_emerald: pass.
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib ipc::tests::ipc_rejects_protocol_mismatch --target-dir target_emerald: pass.
- cargo check -p frankenterm --target-dir target_emerald: pass.
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features mcp --target-dir target_emerald: pass.
Repo-wide gates status:
- cargo fmt --check fails due pre-existing unrelated formatting diffs across many files.
- cargo check --all-targets --target-dir target_emerald: pass.
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings fails due many pre-existing strict-lint issues unrelated to this bead.
Coordination note:
- Encountered repeated concurrent rewrites on target files; resolved via urgent agent-mail coordination and completed atomic reapply.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldHawk","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:44:08.350339Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:11:36.444914Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:11:36.444898Z","close_reason":"Implemented protocol-versioned IPC handshake + contract namespace pathing + error surfacing + targeted validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-1uqi","title":"FTUI-03.2.a Route in-process logs through single output sink","description":"## Background
In-process output must obey one-writer constraints to keep rendering deterministic and avoid output corruption.

## Deliverables
- inventory and removal plan for direct stdout/stderr writes in UI-active paths
- unified output sink routing contract for logs/events/status lines
- verification checks to prove no bypass writes remain

## Acceptance Criteria
- all targeted paths route through the sanctioned sink while UI is active
- checks detect and fail on new bypass writes
- validation evidence includes unit assertions and runtime logs demonstrating routing correctness.,","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmLynx","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:05.996650629Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:12:15.499308732Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:12:15.499172890Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1uqi","depends_on_id":"ft-3cso","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1uqi","depends_on_id":"ft-3g47","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-1utb","title":"FTUI-02.1 Add ftui dependency stack and feature-gated integration path","description":"## Background\nwa must consume /dp/frankentui intentionally, not ad-hoc.\n\n## Deliverables\n- Cargo feature strategy for ftui adoption path\n- path/git pin policy to /dp/frankentui\n- explicit toggles for legacy/new tui runtime\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- project builds with and without ftui migration feature\n- dependency graph is documented and deterministic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderGrove","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:39.031226995Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:18:40.996712962Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:18:40.996571069Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1utb","depends_on_id":"ft-136q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1utb","depends_on_id":"ft-1k52","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
